visits.note: Patient P090 is a 55-year-old male presenting with a 5-year history of Type 2 Diabetes, Hypertension, and Hyperlipidemia. He reports worsening fatigue and increased urination. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 140/90 mmHg and a BMI of 32 kg/m^2. Laboratory values show HbA1c of 7.2%, eGFR of 72 mL/min, and LDL of 130 mg/dL.

Patient P090 returns for follow-up with worsening diabetic control and hypertension. He reports increased shortness of breath and swelling in his legs. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 150/95 mmHg and a BMI of 33 kg/m^2. Laboratory values show HbA1c of 8.5%, eGFR of 60 mL/min, and LDL of 160 mg/dL.

Patient P090 returns for follow-up with continued worsening diabetic control and hypertension. He reports increased chest pain and palpitations. Physical examination reveals a blood pressure of 160/100 mmHg and a BMI of 34 kg/m^2. Laboratory values show HbA1c of 9.0%, eGFR of 45 mL/min, and LDL of 180 mg/dL.
